meta.title: AoD | How to Succeed in the Struggle Against the People's Republic of China (feat. John Lee)
meta.description:
What does success in the struggle against the People's Republic of China look like? Senior Fellow John Lee joins Arsenal of Democracy to deep dive on his report on the topic, co-authored with Lavina Lee, available here: https://www.hudson.org/foreign-policy/success-struggle-against-people-republic-china-john-lavina-lee

Chapters:
0:00 Intro
1:50: “Success” vs. “victory”
4:53: Asia needs the US, and the US needs Asian allies.
7:32: Encouragement vs. coercion of allies
11:08: Strategic expectations of allies
14:47: Australia’s post-WWII strategic posture
18:59: China’s threat to the Indo-Pacific
22:24: The role of New Zealand
27:38: Deterrence is success
29:24: AUKUS and the danger period of the next 5-7 years
33:42: Different conceptions of warfare
34:59: Economic warfare
36:47: Future of US-China economic relationship
40:53: China’s economic decline

John Lee is a senior fellow at Hudson Institute. He is also a senior fellow (non-resident) at the United States Studies Centre and adjunct professor at the University of Sydney.

Arsenal of Democracy is a weekly podcast produced by Hudson Institute and hosted by Marshall Kosloff. Subscribe for the latest episodes and content.

Apple Podcasts: https://podcasts.apple.com/us/podcast/arsenal-of-democracy/id1701812241
Spotify: https://open.spotify.com/show/2DvzfhSJPvZeXrEAFKG3jG
